source_rationale: Both cell types expressed serous secretory markers such as amylase (Amy1), parotid secretory protein (Bpifa2), prolactin induced protein (Pip), and carbonic anhydrase 6 (Car6), but their expression was significantly higher in acinar cells, while Etv1+ cells had higher expression of Krt8, Krt18, and Phlda1 (Figure 2C).

The identified receptor genes enriched in Etv1+ cells included Ghr, Dddr1, St14, Erbb3, and Epha5, which were highly specific to this population (Figure 3B, left panel).

On the other hand, the ligands found in Etv1+ cells were also enriched in other cell types, with the exception of Col7a1, which was highly specific (Figure 3B, right panel).

Thus, they were manually annotated based on expression of a combination of stromal (Col1a12 and Vim) and myoepithelial (Krt14 and Acta2) markers which were highly specific (Figure S1C-D).
Thus, they were manually annotated based on expression of a combination of stromal (Col1a12 and Vim) and myoepithelial (Krt14 and Acta2) markers which were highly specific (Figure S1C-D).
These Etv1+ cells also express Ntrk2 and Erbb3 and thus may respond to myoepithelial cell-derived growth factor ligands.

We did not identify discrete clusters of basal duct cells (Krt14+Krt5+) or peripheral nerves presumably due to limitations in the dissociation technique, which has been previously reported for adult SG tissue dissociation 26.
